personas.name: Average Guy needing Stress Relief
personas.description: Working adult using the app to wind down and sleep better. Converted by a discount trial and now a daily user.
goals: Release daily stress quickly in short sessions
Build a pre-sleep ritual that quiets a busy mind
Access premium content at a price that feels justified
Recommend the app confidently to others
pain_points: Uncertainty about committing to a paid subscription before seeing value
Wants reassurance the app is worth the cost before upgrading
context: Uses the app in the evening, often just before bed
Discovered it through a discounted or free trial offer
May use it during brief breaks in the day for stress relief
Shares recommendations with friends and family
constraints: Price sensitive — discount framing was key to their conversion
Time limited - needs sessions that fit into a short window
Low tolerance for inconvenience; they want to open the app and feel calm quickly

personas.name: The Frustrated High Intent User
personas.description: Therapist referred user who knows exactly what they want but can't navigate to it when already in distress.
goals: Find specific soundscapes or exercises quickly
Reach desired content in as few steps as possible
Trust that the app will retrieve what they need when they are already in distress
pain_points: Cluttered, unclear navigation causes abandonment mid session
Search function fails to surface relevant content
Too many steps between opening the app and reaching useful content
Feels misled by feature advertising that doesn't match the in app experience
Giving up entirely and settling for a less suitable alternative
context: Opens the app specifically when in a state of stress or distress
Has been referred by a therapist or healthcare professional
Knows specific content they want
Has limited patience when already dysregulated
constraints: Cognitive load is high when they open the app, making complexity is especially damaging
Cannot afford to spend time navigating, needs instant access
Trust is fragile, one bad navigation experience can cause permanent damage

personas.name: The Risk averse terster
personas.description: Curious but guarded new user. Won't register or pay until they've seen real value for free.
goals: Evaluate whether the content quality justifies the price before committing
Access meaningful free content without mandatory account creation
Find a clear, honest preview of what premium unlocks
pain_points: Account creation demanded before any real content is accessible
Subscription cost feels unjustified relative to what is previewed
Feels like content quality is hidden behind unnecessary gates
Disconnect between what is advertised and what is freely available
context: Arrives from search or recommendation with genuine interest
Likely comparing multiple wellness apps simultaneously
Will spend time in the free tier before deciding to pay
Reads reviews and pricing carefully before converting
constraints: Will not register an account as their first action, needs to test out first
Budget conscious, needs to feel the price is fair
Low tolerance for dark patterns or bait and switch framing

personas.name: Frustrated Bug, Paying User
personas.description: Active subscriber hitting crashes or ads despite paying. Already committed, trust is decreasing
goals: Open the app reliably without crashes or errors
Experience the premium service they are paying for, ad free
Have tablet or non phone device work as well as a phone
Not have to reinstall or troubleshoot just to use the app
pain_points: App crashes on launch, particularly after an update
Ads appear despite active premium subscription
Tablet instability makes the app unusable on preferred device
No clear resolution path when bugs occur
context: Often uses the app on a tablet for a larger, more relaxing screen
Relies on the app as part of a regular routine
Encounters breakage after app updates
May use the app in family or shared household contexts
constraints: Already paid and has zero tolerance for ads
Not tech savvy enough to diagnose or work around crashes

personas.name: The Vengeful Subscriber
personas.description: Users who was charged unfairly and has been stonewalled by support. Patience is gone and chargeback likely.
goals: Receive a prompt refund or account reinstatement
Get a single clear resolution from support without repeating themselves
Understand exactly what they were charged and why
Feel heard and treated fairly, not like a fraud suspect
pain_points: Charged for a subscription they did not intend to start or could not access
Support repeatedly requests the same information, perceived as deliberate obstruction
Long response times from overseas support teams
Cannot get a straight answer on refund eligibility
Feels the company is prioritizing revenue retention over customer fairness
context: Has already left a one star review or is considering doing so
May be a secondhand user
Has contacted support multiple times across different channels
Is at high risk of initiating a chargeback or public complaint
constraints: Patience is fully exhausted and any further conflicts trigger escalation
May not have access to the original account credentials
Distrust of automated or templated support responses is extremely high
